label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the question carefully. The question is asking what we know about garage sales based on the provided text. First, I'll look at the paragraph that talks about garage sales. The relevant part starts with "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture." So here it's stated that garage sales are part of American culture, which implies they are popular. Next, the text mentions when they are usually held: "These are held usually in the spring and fall when the weather is still good." So option A says it's only in spring, but the text says usually in spring and fall, so A is incorrect. The paragraph also says, "The home owner places items for sale on display... and waits for people to look at what he has to sell." Then it gives examples of items like old magazines, books, paintings, etc. It also mentions that "More than likely the goods on display are in good condition and great buys can be found..." which contradicts option B, which says the goods are totally useless. Option D says all goods are selling far below their real value, but the text states, "If the owner is in a position where he must sell his home or plans to move soon, then he may be forced to practically give things away at a price far below their true worth." This is a conditional statement, not a general rule. So D is incorrect. Option C says
